content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Map findings to WCAG 2.2 criteria, page states, severity rationale, affected users, and human verification without claiming automated conformance.

hints: For Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Defend the Conformance Nonclaim model · Theory Conformance…, name the owner, authorization, source and emitted revision, Node, TypeScript, URL, robots, unknown value, Fetch, Promise, AbortSignal, browser, accessibility, report, handle, recovery, and native-transfer boundary, Conformance Nonclaim precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Defend the Conformance Nonclaim model · Theory Conformance…, trace this concrete input and predict values, errors, blocking, and output before reviewing it: A theory TypeScript team handles case 3918 during a narrow-screen keyboard and screen-reader review. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to map static image, structure, language, semantics, and interaction risks to WCAG with review handoff; every external value remains unknown until runtime admission produces an owned value; then change one duplicate, accessibility state, axe result, resource budget, browser readiness, or manual-review condition without overclaiming. Browser TypeScript uses pure bounded models and original URL, robots, sitemap, HTTP, HTML, frontier, accessibility, browser, and report fixtures only. Real DNS, Fetch, streams, Cheerio, Playwright, axe-core, files, processes, packages, containers, assistive-technology review, load, legal review, restore, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Map findings to WCAG 2.2 criteria, page states, severity rationale, affected users, and human verification without claiming automated conformance.

For Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Defend the Conformance Nonclaim model · Theory Conformance…, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: Zero static findings means the site conforms to WCAG.

checks.description: The cumulative typescript portfolio contains this competency’s named evidence increment.
checks.failureMessage: Add the exact evidence marker // Evidence: crawler-ts-conformance-nonclaim-2opcdl.

checks.pattern: // Evidence: crawler-ts-conformance-nonclaim-2opcdl(?:(?!// Evidence:)[\s\S])*?function\s+evidence_crawler_ts_conformance_nonclaim_2opcdl\s*\([^)]*\)\s*:\s*Readonly<\{[^}]+\}>\s*\{(?=(?:(?!// Evidence:)[\s\S])*?staticChecks < 15)(?=(?:(?!// Evidence:)[\s\S])*?evidencedChecks < 15 \|\| evidencedChecks > staticChecks)(?=(?:(?!// Evidence:)[\s\S])*?reviewCases < 6)(?=(?:(?!// Evidence:)[\s\S])*?conformanceClaims > 0)(?=(?:(?!// Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return)(?:(?!// Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return
checks.description: The increment includes the required executable or reviewable evidence structure.
checks.failureMessage: Complete the full function, prompt contract, or gate record shown in the brief.

checks.description: The evidence note explains the model, failure risk, and verification in the learner’s own words.
checks.failureMessage: Add a causal explanation using findings and wcag plus specific case evidence.
